hard.next_step: Start the NDA process, reconnect over email for due diligence and NDA formalities, schedule the next meeting with CIO Christian (Cody) Dy and CPO Matilde Villaluz including a demo, and plan a face-to-face in Manila; align the CPO conversation around CLM, source-to-contract, AI, and pricing.
hard.ais_why: Ayala Group are pushing for ways to increase AI value

north_star_verdict.headline: Strong 29 May discovery with an engaged CIO, but momentum has thinned: the planned NDA, CPO demo and Manila F2F are all still unscheduled 38 days on, and the biggest threat is no-decision inertia at a young company that says it has 'an opportunity, not a problem.'
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-05-29 (38 days ago), Stage Qualified, 176 days to close (2026-12-29). One transcribed discovery call (2026-05-29); the 2026-05-28 session has no transcript. Next-step actions logged 2026-05-29 (NDA, CPO demo, Manila F2F) remain unactioned. LastActivityDate 2026-06-14 is a rep-side Horizon event invite with no buyer reply, so the last genuine two-way exchange was the 2026-05-29 call. Close date is far out, so timing is not the issue; the issue is stalled next steps and single-threading to the CIO.
evidence: Discovery call 2026-05-29: CIO Christian (Cody) Dy engaged deeply on user experience, AI, ERP integration, logistics references and pricing, and asked to schedule a demo ('Yeah, let's schedule it')
Cody framed the initiative as opportunity-led, not pain-led: 'I'm not coming in this discussion with a problem. It's more of an opportunity'
Cody confirmed a live CLM procurement in parallel: 'We're currently sourcing for a contract for us for CLM as well'
Cody committed the CLM timeline to this year: 'CLM part is likely to happen this year... it's gonna happen this year'
Planned next steps (NDA, demo with CPO Matilde Villaluz, F2F in Manila) logged 2026-05-29 are all still unscheduled
Only touch since the call is a 2026-06-14 rep-side Horizon Singapore invite emailed to Cody and Matilde with no logged reply
Single-threaded to the CIO; CPO Matilde Villaluz (primary contact, procurement owner) has not yet joined a session on this deal
Amount sized $150k and 2026-12-29 close are credible for an early CLM-led, multi-module new-business deal in a 12-15 month cycle

metrics.narrative: Pain is articulated qualitatively but nothing is quantified. On the 2026-05-29 call CIO Christian (Cody) Dy described AC Logistics as 'fairly young' (created by Ayala around 2022, having absorbed Air21 and other firms into 8-9 business units) with procurement processes that are 'basic' and must scale, saying 'we're not coming in from a problem, but I don't want it to be a problem eventually.' He stressed that 'procurement's pretty key to logistics' (sourcing truckers, warehouses, raw materials). No cost, cycle-time, savings or headcount figure was stated. The move is to co-build a quantified value case (contract cycle time, tail-spend savings via agentic AI, supplier onboarding speed) during the demo so deferral carries a measurable cost.

economic_buyer.narrative: No budget owner is confirmed. CIO Christian (Cody) Dy (Decision Maker) drives the systems evaluation and controls the CLM/IT decision, and CPO Matilde Villaluz is the SF primary contact and the natural procurement budget authority, but she has not yet joined a call on this deal. Cody surfaced two external funding signals on 2026-05-29: 'APAP Molar will be investing on iContract' (a new investor in the entity) and repeated references to iALA corporate, either of which could hold the purse. Zycus (Carl Kimball) set the pricing lever on the vendor side but the buyer-side economic buyer is not nailed down. The move is to confirm, during the CPO session, who signs for a CLM/source-to-contract purchase across the CIO, CPO, APAP Molar and iALA corporate.

decision_criteria.narrative: Cody named clear evaluation criteria on 2026-05-29: (1) ease of use for non-technical warehouse users, his explicit priority ('the user experience... that has always been my pet peeve'); (2) native/agentic AI, aligned to the group-wide AI push captured in AIS_Why__c; (3) integration with the existing SAP ERP without lock-in ('most of your modules are in competition with SAP and Oracle... or can it complement'); (4) depth of source-to-contract functionality; (5) proven logistics references (he asked 'do you have any clients who are in the logistics space'); and (6) modular, affordable pricing for a young, scaling business. These are buyer-stated, so the criteria are confirmed; what remains is proving each in a tailored demo.

decision_process.narrative: The near-term process Cody laid out on 2026-05-29 is: execute an NDA and due diligence, then a demo that includes CPO Matilde Villaluz, then a face-to-face in Manila with Carl and Akshay. Cody offered to 'connect everyone again', so he controls access to the CPO. A separate, live CLM procurement is running in parallel ('we're currently sourcing for a contract for us for CLM as well'), which is the formal process Zycus must get named into. The full approval chain (CIO, CPO, APAP Molar investor, iALA corporate) is not yet mapped. The move is to agree the evaluation steps and decision owners in writing at the CPO session.

paper_process.narrative: This is new-business contracting at the very front: the first paper step is the mutual NDA, which Zycus offered to share ('We'll share our NDA') and Cody agreed to progress ('let's start with NDA... the due diligence, NDAs'). No MSA, Order Form or SOW is in play yet, consistent with a Qualified-stage deal. As a Philippines/APAC new-business deal the eventual motion would run MSA plus Order Form and a separately signed SOW, but that is far out. There is no Current_Contract_Expiration forcing function on record. Visibility improves once the NDA is signed and the parallel CLM procurement's timeline and paper requirements are shared.

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is confirmed qualitatively and owned by the CIO. Cody described immature, 'basic' procurement processes at a young logistics group that 'threw out all the legacy systems' after acquisitions and now needs structured sourcing for truckers, warehouses and raw materials to scale, plus poor legacy-ERP usability for warehouse users who 'might not be totally digitally savvy.' He explicitly wants to avoid procurement becoming a problem as the business scales ('I don't want it to be a problem eventually'). This is genuine, buyer-stated pain, but because he frames it as future-proofing rather than an acute break, the risk is that it is not urgent enough to force a decision.

champion.narrative: A champion is developing but not secured. CIO Christian (Cody) Dy, the Decision Maker, drove the 2026-05-29 discovery, asked detailed Zycus-specific questions, requested a demo, committed the CLM to 'this year', and volunteered to bring in and connect CPO Matilde Villaluz. What has developed: genuine enthusiasm and control of access. What is still missing: evidence of internal advocacy, no introduction yet to the budget owner (APAP Molar / iALA corporate), and no pushback on alternatives. CPO Matilde Villaluz (SF primary) reportedly already knows Zycus ('she already met you guys... she immediately replied') but has not engaged on this deal. The move is to convert Cody into an active champion by arming him with the ROI narrative, logistics references and pricing at the CPO demo.

competition.narrative: No rival is buyer-preferred and none was named as a shortlisted CLM contender on 2026-05-29. SF Competitors__c lists Coupa and SAP Ariba, but the buyer only referenced SAP/Oracle as ERP incumbents he integrates with, not as procurement front-runners, and Carl positioned Ariba as outdated. The real competitive dynamics are (1) status-quo / no-decision inertia, given Cody's 'opportunity, not a problem' framing, and (2) a live parallel CLM procurement whose other bidders are undisclosed ('we're currently sourcing for a contract for us for CLM'). The wedge is embedded agentic AI (Merlin Intake in Teams), logistics references (SIRVA, Bidvest) and modular pricing for a young business; the priority is to get named on the CLM shortlist and give deferral a cost.

deal_movement.summary: One strong discovery call on 2026-05-29 opened the deal (stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified, amount sized $100k to $150k), but the logged next steps have not advanced and the only touch since is a 2026-06-14 rep-side event invite with no buyer reply. Last genuine two-way exchange: the 2026-05-29 call.

items.change: Discovery call with CIO Cody Dy: strong interest in CLM + source-to-contract, demo requested, CPO to be looped in
Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified and amount sized $100k to $150k by Carl Kimball
Horizon Singapore event invite emailed to Cody and Matilde; no buyer reply logged

competitive_position.summary: The strongest current threat is no-decision / status-quo inertia, not a vendor: on 2026-05-29 Cody said 'I'm not coming in this discussion with a problem, it's more of an opportunity,' so a young company that just threw out its legacy systems can defer indefinitely. Second is an undisclosed parallel CLM procurement Cody confirmed is already running. Coupa and SAP Ariba are on the SF Competitors field but neither is buyer-preferred; SAP/Oracle appear only as ERP incumbents Zycus integrates with, and Ariba was positioned by Zycus as outdated. Recency-weighted, the live contest (Jul 2026) is inertia plus the unnamed CLM bidders, with Coupa/Ariba dormant.

competitors.how_we_win: Get named on the CLM shortlist, then widen the frame from CLM to Zycus source-to-contract + Merlin Intake so the single-module bid becomes a platform decision.

competitors.how_we_win: Lead with embedded agentic AI depth (Merlin/Anna), logistics references (SIRVA, Bidvest) and modular pricing flexibility for a young, scaling business.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is well aligned to the buyer's stated criteria on UX, AI and ERP integration; the open exposure is pricing, which has not yet been landed for a budget-conscious young company.

best_practice_check.summary: Early Qualified new-business deal with a strong CIO opening; the win levers now are multi-threading to the budget owner, beating no-decision inertia with a quantified case, and getting named on the live CLM procurement.
flags: Multi-thread to CPO Matilde Villaluz now: she owns procurement and the deal is single-threaded to the CIO after one call.
Beat no-decision inertia: convert Cody's 'opportunity, not a problem' framing into a quantified future-proofing/scaling case tied to the CLM 'this year' timeline.
Get named on the live parallel CLM procurement and map who signs (CIO, CPO, APAP Molar investor, iALA corporate).

The AI story landed as interesting on 2026-05-29 but is not yet validated in a tailored demo or priced, so it is well positioned but unproven.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 8.0 / Moderate ('Ayala Group are pushing for ways to increase AI value'). The 2026-05-29 call corroborates real AI curiosity (Cody asked how AI pricing works and engaged on Merlin Intake) without a committed AI mandate, so the field read is accurate, not under-rated.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (basic processes, scaling young logistics group, ERP usability); no cost, cycle-time or savings figures quantified.

critical_signals.text: The 29 May discovery went well, with CIO Cody Dy engaging on UX, AI and pricing and asking to schedule a demo, but nothing has been booked in the 38 days since.
No rival is buyer-preferred; the real contest is no-decision inertia at a young company that says it has 'an opportunity, not a problem.'
Cody confirmed a live parallel CLM procurement is already running, which Zycus must get named on before it hardens around another vendor.
CPO Matilde Villaluz owns procurement and is expected in the next session but has not yet engaged on this deal.
Pricing is unlanded: Cody asked how AI pricing works and Carl offered indicative CLM/source-to-contract costing, but none has been sent.

scope_change.detail: Amount was sized up from $100k to $150k at creation on 2026-05-29 as the scope widened from CLM/intake to a source-to-contract multi-module footprint; no narrowing observed.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Early Qualified CLM-led deal at a young Ayala logistics unit: CIO Cody Dy is engaged and wants a demo, but he called it 'an opportunity, not a problem,' the CPO who likely owns budget has not joined, and the biggest threat is no-decision inertia, not a rival.

win_position.text: We hold a genuine early opening: CIO Christian (Cody) Dy drove the 29 May discovery, asked Zycus-specific questions on UX, AI and pricing, and asked to schedule a demo with the CPO (Avoma, 29 May).
Cody framed this as 'an opportunity, not a problem' (Avoma, 29 May), so no-decision inertia, not a competitor, is the top thing that can lose it.
We are single-threaded to the CIO; CPO Matilde Villaluz, the likely procurement budget owner, has never joined a call on this deal and the economic buyer is unconfirmed.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has thinned since 29 May: the planned NDA, CPO demo and Manila F2F are all still unscheduled and the only touch since is an unanswered 14 Jun Horizon invite.
The customer pulled the next step, with Cody saying 'let's schedule it' for the demo and offering to connect the CPO (Avoma, 29 May).

customer_commitment.text: Cody committed the CLM to a timeframe ('it's gonna happen this year') and volunteered to loop in CPO Matilde Villaluz (Avoma, 29 May).
Commitment is verbal only: no NDA signed, no demo booked, and broader procurement stays exploratory ('anything procurement part, we're still exploring').

deal_risk.text: Status-quo / no-decision is the primary risk: a young company that 'threw out all the legacy systems' and feels no acute pain can defer indefinitely.
A live parallel CLM procurement is running with undisclosed bidders, and Zycus is not yet confirmed on that shortlist (Avoma, 29 May).

deal_risk.evidence: No-decision inertia plus an undisclosed parallel CLM procurement, with the economic buyer unconfirmed.

deal_momentum.evidence: Logged next steps (NDA, CPO demo, F2F) unactioned 38 days on; only touch is an unanswered event invite.
